steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The text opens by declaring Julius was "very useful" and then strings together a list of specific skills and areas of knowledge: the neighborhood, roads, watercourses, soils, hunting and fishing spots, and the management of horses and dogs. The whole passage is a claim plus an enumeration of supporting examples.

steps.title: Determine the purpose
steps.content: Because the sentences exist to catalog the different domains in which Julius proved helpful, the purpose is to explain the various ways he was useful to the narrator's household.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• "To compare the narrator's reaction to a new home with his wife's reaction": the wife is never described as having a reaction; this overreaches beyond the text. ✗
  • "To give an example of Julius's knowledge about soil": soil is only one item in a long list, so this misreads the scope as a single example. ✗
  • "To show that the narrator and Julius often hunt and fish together": the passage says Julius knew where the best hunting and fishing were, not that he and the narrator did either together. ✗
  • "To explain different ways in which Julius was helpful": directly captures the claim-plus-enumeration structure. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
